text: Thermal cycling unit 7 failed last night — chassis temp probe at the motor mount hit 91°C at full-load cycle 142. Spec is 85°C max. Pulling unit for teardown this morning.
Adding @sarah and @raj — looks load-side, not cell-side. Margin shrinking. Need root cause before DVT exit review next week.
Looking now. Chassis bracket has limited heat-spreading area where the motor flange clamps. Could be conduction-limited, not airflow. Will measure with the IR cam.

Heads-up: aluminum extrusion AL-6063-T5 supplier (ExtruCo) issued a force majeure this morning — labor strike at the Akron mill, indefinite duration. We have ~3 weeks of inventory at current build rate. Sourcing alternates.

Reached out to two alternates (MetalPro, AlumWest). MetalPro can meet our spec but lead time is 6 weeks. AlumWest 3 weeks but tolerance band is +/- 0.15mm vs our +/- 0.10mm. ECO needed if we go AlumWest.
Both items are now on the risk register. Daily standup at 4pm. Sarah owns thermal root cause; Mei owns sourcing path. Need a recommended ECO direction by EOD Thursday.

Firmware regression: motor driver F-state recovery on overcurrent trip is hitting an off-by-one in the retry counter. Drives go into latch-off after 3 retries instead of the spec'd 5. Patch in PR #214.
Will retest with Lin's thermal rig once #214 merges. If the overcurrent is being triggered by the thermal margin compression, we may be chasing one root cause not two.
FYI gripper-motor mount also uses an extrusion piece from the same family. If ExtruCo strike drags, we may need to qualify a second source on that part too.

Teardown shows discoloration on the bracket boss face directly under the motor flange. Heat is concentrated, not distributed. Matches Sarah's hypothesis. Drop test on unit 5 still passing — keeping that on schedule.
